name: vercel
description: Inspect Vercel projects, deployments, and environment variables

tools.name: List Projects
tools.description: List projects of the authenticated user or team

from.description: Continuation token (pagination.next from a previous response)

teamId.description: Team ID to perform the request on behalf of (omit for personal account)

historicalSummary.omitted: build settings, latest deployment payloads, and provider-specific fields

tools.name: Get Project
tools.description: Get a project by ID or name

teamId.description: Team ID to perform the request on behalf of

tools.name: List Deployments
tools.description: List deployments under the authenticated user or team

projectId.description: Filter deployments by project ID or name

state.description: Filter by state: BUILDING, ERROR, INITIALIZING, QUEUED, READY, CANCELED, BLOCKED

since.description: Only deployments created after this JavaScript timestamp (ms)

until.description: Only deployments created before this JavaScript timestamp (ms)

teamId.description: Team ID to perform the request on behalf of

historicalSummary.omitted: git metadata, checks, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Deployment
tools.description: Get a deployment by ID or hostname URL

idOrUrl.description: Deployment ID (dpl_...) or hostname, e.g. my-app-abc123.vercel.app

teamId.description: Team ID to perform the request on behalf of

tools.name: List Environment Variables
tools.description: List the environment variables of a project (values stay encrypted unless decrypt is set)

gitBranch.description: Filter to variables for a preview git branch

decrypt.description: Set to 'true' to decrypt variable values in the response

teamId.description: Team ID to perform the request on behalf of

prompts.title: Latest deployments
prompts.prompt: List my most recent Vercel deployments with their state and target environment.

prompts.title: Find failed deployments
prompts.prompt: Find my Vercel deployments in ERROR state from the last day and tell me which projects are affected.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Vercel API Setup

steps.title: Sign in to Vercel
steps.description: Go to https://vercel.com and sign in (the free Hobby plan is sufficient for testing).

steps.title: Create an access token
steps.description: Open Account Settings > Tokens (https://vercel.com/account/settings/tokens), create a token, choose its scope (personal account or a team), and copy it.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Add the token to your .env as VERCEL_TOKEN=...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Projects. For team resources, pass the team's ID via the teamId parameter (find it in Team Settings).

notes: To access resources owned by a team, every call must include teamId (or slug) as a query parameter
Endpoint versions differ per route (v10 projects, v7 deployments list, v13 deployment detail) — this is normal for the Vercel API
